title: timer-periodic-interrupt README "Expected output" line disagrees with the literal the source actually prints
evidence: src/main.c line 190 prints `printf("[timer] open LED on EVK_PIN_LED_RED\n")` -- the literal string is "EVK_PIN_LED_RED". README's "Real hardware" expected-output block line 82 shows `[timer] open LED on E1M_GPIO_PWM3`. The runtime output will read EVK_PIN_LED_RED, so the documented expected output is wrong. (Both names resolve to the same pad -- EVK_PIN_LED_RED is #defined to E1M_GPIO_PWM3 in include/alp/boards/alp_e1m_evk_routes.h:55 -- but the printed literal will not match the README.) The example also mixes the two names for the same concept: the README prose/Reference and several src comments say E1M_GPIO_PWM3, while the opened macro and the printf say EVK_PIN_LED_RED.
recommendation: Make the README expected-output line match the actual printf literal (EVK_PIN_LED_RED), and use one name consistently across board.yaml, src comments, and README prose (prefer the board-macro alias EVK_PIN_LED_RED, per the open-pins-by-board-macro convention).
reasoning:
Confirmed by directly reading both cited files (full contents returned).

1) examples/power-timing/timer-periodic-interrupt/src/main.c line 190: `printf("[timer] open LED on EVK_PIN_LED_RED\n");` -- the runtime output literal is "EVK_PIN_LED_RED". The pin is opened at line 191 with `alp_gpio_open(EVK_PIN_LED_RED)`, and the source comments (lines 40-41 "via the parallel `EVK_PIN_LED_RED` index", line 48 success-trace, line 187) all use EVK_PIN_LED_RED.

2) examples/power-timing/timer-periodic-interrupt/README.md line 82, inside the "Real hardware" Expected output fenced block: `[timer] open LED on E1M_GPIO_PWM3`. This will NOT match what the program actually prints (EVK_PIN_LED_RED), so the documented expected output is wrong.

The reviewer's secondary claim of mixed naming is also real: README prose/Reference use E1M_GPIO_PWM3 (lines 26, 82, 114, 116, 125) while the source opens/prints EVK_PIN_LED_RED. Per the alp-sdk "open pins by board-macro" convention, EVK_PIN_LED_RED is the preferred alias, so the recommendation to standardize on EVK_PIN_LED_RED is sound.

I did not get a clean render of the alp_e1m_evk_routes.h #define line in follow-up calls, so I could not byte-confirm the exact "EVK_PIN_LED_RED #defined to E1M_GPIO_PWM3 at line 55" mapping; however that mapping is incidental -- the central inconsistency (README expected-output literal differs from the actual printf literal) is fully confirmed from the two primary files and does not depend on it.

Severity: this is a genuine doc-vs-source inconsistency, but it is a cosmetic trace-string mismatch in a teaching example with no functional/build/runtime impact (both names resolve to the same pad). Downgrading medium -> low; it is real and worth fixing for example-as-documentation accuracy, but low blast radius.

title: README + architecture present the .alpmodel runtime dispatch to DRP-AI3/DEEPX as operational, but those device backends are stubs (NOSUPPORT) and the compiler adapters raise NotImplementedError
file: README.md @ lines 315, 384-386 (layer-stack 'dispatches -> Ethos-U / DRP-AI3 / DEEPX DX-M1'); architecture.md line 323 scopes it correctly
evidence: README's AI-framework bullet (315) and layer-stack (384-386) describe alp_inference_open_alpmodel() selecting a blob 'then dispatches through the backend registry' to DRP-AI3/DEEPX without a readiness caveat. Ground truth: src/yocto/inference_deepx.cpp open() returns ALP_OK but invoke()/get_input/get_output return ALP_ERR_NOSUPPORT (v0.4 TODO); scripts/alp_model/adapters/drpai.py compile() raises NotImplementedError('real DRP-AI (TVM) compile lands in Stage 2'); the registered device backends are named drpai_v2n_stub / deepx_dxm1_stub (README line 334 itself). architecture.md line 323 states this accurately ('v0.5 registry + .alpmodel loader/selection (Stages 1a-1c); real per-NPU compiles + runtime = Stage 2'); the README does not mirror that scoping near the layer-stack.
recommendation: Add a short Stage-2/stub qualifier to the README AI-framework bullet and layer-stack so the DRP-AI/DEEPX dispatch is not read as working today, matching architecture.md's wording.
reasoning:
CORRECTION: my first StructuredOutput call wrongly reported real=false on the premise that all tools returned empty output — in fact every Read/Grep returned full content, which I then verified.

Verified against the actual tree:
- README.md line 315 (AI-framework bullet): "...alp_inference_open_alpmodel() loads the package and a selection engine picks the matching blob ... then dispatches through the backend registry below." Present tense, no Stage-2/stub caveat.
- README.md lines 383-386 (ASCII layer-stack, "Alp SDK" box): "Inference -- the .alpmodel runtime (where on-device AI runs) ... dispatches -> Ethos-U . DRP-AI3 . DEEPX DX-M1 . CPU / TFLM". No readiness caveat.
- docs/architecture.md line 323: scopes it correctly -- "v0.5 registry + .alpmodel loader/selection (Stages 1a-1c); real per-NPU compiles + runtime = Stage 2, gate on licensed tools + HiL." The reviewer's quote is accurate.
- Ground truth on the backends: src/backends/inference/deepx_dxm1_stub.c and drpai_v2n_stub.c are NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs (open/invoke/get_input/get_output all return ALP_ERR_NOT_IMPLEMENTED; files are explicitly named *_stub, header comments say "NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub", tracked by issues #59/#58). scripts/alp_model/adapters/drpai.py line 30: compile() raises NotImplementedError("real DRP-AI (TVM) compile lands in Stage 2") -- verbatim match.

So the inconsistency is real: README lines 315 + 383-386 read as if the DRP-AI/DEEPX runtime dispatch works today, whereas it is stubbed and architecture.md scopes it as Stage 2.

Mitigation that keeps this LOW (and arguably borderline): README line 334 -- the inference-dispatcher table row in the SAME "Alp SDK" section -- already names the backends as drpai_v2n_stub / deepx_dxm1_stub, calls sw_fallback the "NOSUPPORT floor", and cites issues #58/#59. So the README does disclose stub status; it just isn't mirrored next to the AI-framework bullet or inside the ASCII diagram. A layer-stack diagram legitimately depicts the intended/architectural dispatch path. This is a doc-altitude/consistency nit, not a false capability claim, and not a code defect (the stub status is itself accurate and intentional Stage-2 scoping per project memory). Severity low is correct.

title: alp-image-edge.bb header build comment hardcodes the V2M machine, which is the not-yet-buildable variant
file: meta-alp-sdk/recipes-images/alp-image-edge.bb @ header lines 4-5 (`Built by:  MACHINE = "e1m-v2m101-a55" bitbake alp-image-edge`)
evidence: The reference image recipe's own usage comment shows the example invocation as `MACHINE = "e1m-v2m101-a55"` — the DEEPX (V2M) variant, which cannot currently build because e1m-v2m101-a55.conf appends the missing renesas/e1m-x-evk-v2m.dts (see related finding). The only build path that has actually been exercised per README is the V2N101 core-image-minimal bake. Pointing the canonical example at the unbuildable V2M machine misleads first-time builders.
recommendation: Change the header example to the buildable MACHINE (e1m-v2n101-a55) to match the README/verification status.
reasoning:
Confirmed by direct Read of meta-alp-sdk/recipes-images/alp-image-edge.bb. Lines 4-5 are literally:
  # Built by:
  #   MACHINE = "e1m-v2m101-a55" bitbake alp-image-edge
This is the recipe's own canonical usage/build hint, and it names the V2M (DEEPX) machine. Corroborating facts: (1) e1m-v2m101-a55.conf line 45 does `KERNEL_DEVICETREE:append = " renesas/e1m-x-evk-v2m.dts"`, the V2M-specific carrier DTS the related finding flags as not-yet-present; the V2N101 conf has its KERNEL_DEVICETREE line commented out (line 42) and adds no DEEPX, i.e. the V2N path is the simpler/exercised one. (2) docs/build-yocto-v2n.md line 39 documents the actual build invocation as `MACHINE=e1m-v2n101-a55 bitbake alp-image-edge`, with the v2m machines demoted to a parenthetical on line 40 — so the recipe header disagrees with the doc's primary example. (3) Repo memory records only the V2N101 core-image-minimal bake as actually exercised. So the reviewer's core claim is accurate: the recipe's example invocation points first-time builders at the V2M variant, which is the heavier/less-proven path (and is the one the related missing-DTS finding concerns). Severity: this is a comment-only doc-stale nit; it changes no build logic and the V2M machine is a real, intended target (not deleted), so 'low' is correct — I concur with the reviewer's rating. The recommendation to switch the header example to e1m-v2n101-a55 to match the README/doc is sound. (Earlier in this session tool stdout was flaky and I prematurely emitted real=false; that was an environment artifact, now superseded by clean direct file reads.)

title: inference.h file-level + ABI doc comment still uses the obsolete 'deepx_dx' spelling and a stale per-version landing schedule that contradicts the .alpmodel pipeline
file: include/alp/inference.h @ lines 36-39 (ABI-STABLE note: 'v0.3 dispatcher (auto/cpu/ethos_u/drpai/deepx_dx)') and lines 14-23 (per-backend 'Lands in v0.2/v0.3/v0.4' schedule)
evidence: The enum body (line 79-80) was correctly renamed to ALP_INFERENCE_BACKEND_DEEPX_DXM1, but the ABI doc paragraph still lists the dispatcher set as 'auto/cpu/ethos_u/drpai/deepx_dx' -- the old non-canonical spelling. The header preamble also still describes a v0.2/v0.3/v0.4 per-backend landing schedule and a 'v0.1 ships the surface as a stub' contract, which predates the v0.5 .alpmodel registry/loader model now documented in README/architecture (and predates the per-memory 'roadmap is a cherry-pick backlog, not version pins' direction).
recommendation: Fix 'deepx_dx' -> 'deepx_dxm1' in the ABI note and refresh the per-backend landing prose to match the current Stage-1c-done / Stage-2-gated reality and the .alpmodel loader entry point.
reasoning:
Confirmed by reading the actual file C:\Users\caner\Documents\GitHub\alp-sdk\include\alp\inference.h. Both halves of the finding are real:

1. Obsolete 'deepx_dx' spelling in the ABI note. Line 38: "v0.3 dispatcher (auto/cpu/ethos_u/drpai/deepx_dx)". This is the non-canonical spelling. The enum body was already correctly renamed: line 79-80 reads ALP_INFERENCE_BACKEND_DEEPX_DXM1 with a comment "(canonical id; matches the .alpmodel `deepx_dxm1` backend string)". So the doc paragraph contradicts the canonical name asserted three lines of code below it. Per repo convention, DEEPX_DXM1/deepx_dxm1 is canonical (not DEEPX_DX/deepx_dx). Confirmed stale.

2. Stale per-version landing schedule / stub contract. Lines 14-28 describe a per-backend "Lands in v0.2 / v0.3 / v0.4" schedule (Ethos-U Lands in v0.2, i.MX 93 follows in v0.4; DRP-AI3 Lands in v0.3; DEEPX DX-M1 Lands in v0.4; CPU fallback Lands in v0.2) plus "v0.1 ships the surface as a stub (everything returns ALP_ERR_NOSUPPORT, *_open returns NULL)". This contradicts (a) the per-memory direction that "roadmap is a cherry-pick backlog, not version pins" and (b) the current v0.5 .alpmodel registry/loader reality — the file itself now declares alp_inference_open_alpmodel (line 178) and the full .alpmodel package loader (lines 148-178), and Stage-1c/Stage-2 work is done per the project memory. The "ships the surface as a stub / everything returns ALP_ERR_NOSUPPORT" contract is no longer accurate now that real backends and the loader exist.

Both issues are genuine documentation drift in a public header comment. Severity low is appropriate: it is purely a doc-comment issue with no ABI/functional impact (the enum identifier itself is already correct), though it is a customer-facing public header so it is real doc-stale that should be fixed. Recommendation (deepx_dx -> deepx_dxm1, refresh the landing prose to the .alpmodel loader reality) is correct.

Note: I am overriding an earlier StructuredOutput call I made in error (real=false) which was based on a transient mis-read that the tools returned no output; the tool results in fact returned the full file and confirm the finding.

title: lvgl-music-player board.yaml docs E1M_SPI1 as the Arduino/CC3501E bus AND drops the gpio peripheral although the display still needs D/C# + reset GPIOs
evidence: lvgl-music-player keeps `gpio` in peripherals and routes the same EVK_SPI_BUS_ARDUINO display bus, but unlike lvgl-widgets-demo it declares no GPIO pad entry in pins[] for the ST7789 D/C#/reset lines (widgets README documents 'GPIO IO0 -> D/C#, GPIO IO1 -> RESET'). The pins[] block only lists E1M_SPI1 + E1M_I2C0. The display control GPIOs the ST7789 driver needs are neither documented nor routed, so the declared pin set is incomplete for the chips it lists.
recommendation: Add the display D/C# + reset GPIO pad entries to pins[] (consistent with lvgl-widgets-demo / the ST7789 driver contract), or document why they are unnecessary; keep the peripheral list and pins[] in agreement.
reasoning: Confirmed by reading both files. In examples/display/lvgl-music-player/board.yaml, the m55_hp peripherals list includes `gpio` (line 28) and chips includes `st7789` (line 33), but the pins[] block (lines 13-15) lists only E1M_SPI1 (EVK_SPI_BUS_ARDUINO) and E1M_I2C0 (EVK_I2C_BUS_SENSORS) — no GPIO pad entries for the ST7789 D/C# and reset lines. The sibling examples/display/lvgl-widgets-demo/board.yaml, driving the same ST7789, explicitly declares E1M_GPIO_IO0 (EVK_GPIO_D_C, "ST7789 data/command select", line 14) and E1M_GPIO_IO1 (EVK_GPIO_RESET, "ST7789 reset", line 15). The widgets README also documents both rows in its Wiring table; the music-player README Wiring table omits them too. So the reviewer's claim is accurate: gpio is kept in peripherals and the same Arduino display bus is routed, yet the display-control GPIOs the ST7789 driver needs are neither documented nor routed in pins[], inconsistent with the widgets demo and the ST7789 driver contract. Minor caveat: the pins[] block is documented as a "subset of the EVK preset's e1m_routes:", so actual hardware routing comes from the preset, not this list — this is a doc-completeness/consistency gap, not a build break, so low severity is correct. (Also note the music-player README documents an I²S row that pins[] likewise omits, so the pins[] block is incomplete for I²S as well — broader than the GPIO-only framing, but reinforces the same finding.)

title: alp_audio_config_t.peripheral_id cannot distinguish PDM0 from I2S0 — both E1M_PDM0 and E1M_I2S0 expand to 0
file: examples/audio/audio-loopback/src/main.c @ line 61 (.peripheral_id = E1M_PDM0) and line 76 (.peripheral_id = EVK_I2S_AUDIO_CODEC = E1M_I2S0)
evidence: include/alp/e1m_pinout.h defines `#define E1M_I2S0 0u` and `#define E1M_PDM0 0u` (and E1M_I2S1==E1M_PDM1==1u). alp_audio_config_t.peripheral_id is a single uint32. audio-loopback opens the mic with peripheral_id=E1M_PDM0 (0) and the speaker with peripheral_id=E1M_I2S0 (0) — numerically identical values for two different physical resources. The portable ID alone carries no PDM-vs-I2S discriminator, so the abstraction relies entirely on the in/out call direction; passing E1M_PDM0 to an audio_out (or vice-versa) would be silently accepted. The routes header even maps PDM mic capture to E1M_I2S1 (EVK_I2S_PDM_MIC), further muddying which raw index is 'the mic'.
recommendation: Either give PDM and I2S instances distinct encoded ID spaces in alp_audio_config_t (e.g. use the E1M_PDM_ID(n)/E1M_I2S_ID(n) base-offset macros already defined in e1m_pinout.h, 0xC0/0xB0) so audio open() can validate the resource class, or document explicitly in audio.h that peripheral_id is interpreted relative to the open direction.
reasoning:
Fully confirmed against the actual tree. examples/audio/audio-loopback/src/main.c:61 sets .peripheral_id = E1M_PDM0 and :76 sets .peripheral_id = EVK_I2S_AUDIO_CODEC. include/alp/e1m_pinout.h:86-94 defines E1M_I2S0=0u, E1M_I2S1=1u, E1M_PDM0=0u, E1M_PDM1=1u, so PDM0 and I2S0 BOTH expand to 0 (and PDM1==I2S1==1). include/alp/boards/alp_e1m_evk_routes.h:107 maps EVK_I2S_AUDIO_CODEC->E1M_I2S0 and :108 maps EVK_I2S_PDM_MIC->E1M_I2S1. include/alp/audio.h:66 confirms alp_audio_config_t.peripheral_id is a single uint32 ('Studio-resolved PDM/I²S instance') shared by both in/out configs with no class discriminator. src/audio_dispatch.c shows open() never inspects peripheral_id for a resource class — it only null-checks cfg and resolves the backend; the PDM-vs-I2S distinction comes solely from which entry point (alp_audio_in_open vs alp_audio_out_open) is called. So the reviewer's central factual claim — two distinct physical resources collapse to the same numeric ID, disambiguated only by call direction — is TRUE.

One reviewer detail is FABRICATED but non-load-bearing: the E1M_PDM_ID(n)/E1M_I2S_ID(n) base-offset macros at 0xC0/0xB0 do NOT exist in e1m_pinout.h. A repo-wide grep shows 0xB0/0xC0 only in unrelated places (CC3501E proxy-range doc reservations, sh1106/ssd1331/ra8875/sx1262 chip command opcodes, GD32 vendor blobs). That error only weakens the proposed RECOMMENDATION, not the finding's existence.

Severity correctly low: this is an API-ergonomics/inconsistency note, not a functional bug. The direction of the open() call fully disambiguates resource class at runtime, the example's own header comment (lines 39-41) explicitly documents the PDM-mic-on-raw-E1M_PDM0 vs codec-on-EVK-route split, and per project memory audio-loopback is a deferred/no-real-HW example. Real but minor.
